description: PM-KISAN is a Central Sector scheme with 100% funding from Government of India. It provides income support to all landholding farmer families across the country to enable them to take care of expenses related to agriculture and allied activities as well as domestic needs.
benefits: Financial benefit of Rs. 6,000/- per year in three equal installments of Rs. 2,000/- each, every four months, directly transferred to bank accounts of beneficiary farmers.
required_documents: Aadhaar Card, Land Records/Khasra/Khatauni, Bank Account Passbook
application_steps: 1. Visit pmkisan.gov.in or nearest CSC. 2. Fill registration form with land details. 3. Submit Aadhaar and bank details. 4. Verification by State/UT authorities. 5. Benefit credited to bank account.

scheme_name: Ayushman Bharat - Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PM-JAY)
description: PM-JAY is the world's largest health insurance scheme fully financed by the Government of India. It provides health cover of Rs. 5 lakhs per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization to over 12 crore poor and vulnerable families.
benefits: Health cover of Rs. 5,00,000 per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization. Cashless and paperless access to services at empanelled public and private hospitals.
required_documents: Aadhaar Card, Ration Card/SECC Data, Family ID
application_steps: 1. Check eligibility on pmjay.gov.in using mobile/Aadhaar. 2. Visit nearest Ayushman Mitra at empanelled hospital. 3. Get Ayushman card generated. 4. Avail cashless treatment at empanelled hospitals.

description: PMAY-G aims to provide pucca house with basic amenities to all houseless households and households living in kutcha and dilapidated houses in rural areas by 2024. It is a flagship scheme of the Ministry of Rural Development.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs. 1,20,000 in plain areas and Rs. 1,30,000 in hilly/difficult areas for construction of pucca house with toilet, LPG connection, electricity and drinking water.
required_documents: Aadhaar Card, SECC Data, Bank Account Passbook, Job Card (if applicable)
application_steps: 1. Selection through Gram Sabha from SECC 2011 data. 2. Verification by Block Development Officer. 3. Fund release in installments to bank account. 4. Geo-tagging of house construction.

description: PMAY-U aims to provide housing for all in urban areas by providing central assistance to implementing agencies for construction of pucca houses to all eligible beneficiaries.
benefits: Central Assistance of Rs. 1.5 lakh to Rs. 2.5 lakh depending on category. Interest subsidy up to 6.5% on home loans under CLSS component.
required_documents: Aadhaar Card, Income Certificate, Address Proof, Bank Account Details
application_steps: 1. Apply online at pmay-urban.gov.in. 2. Submit income and identity documents. 3. Verification by ULB/State. 4. Allotment under BLC/AHP/ISSR/CLSS component.

scheme_name: PM Street Vendor's AtmaNirbhar Nidhi (PM SVANidhi)
description: PM SVANidhi is a special micro-credit facility scheme to provide affordable working capital loan to street vendors to resume their livelihoods that were adversely affected due to COVID-19 lockdown.
benefits: Working capital loan up to Rs. 10,000. Interest subsidy of 7% per annum. No collateral required. Digital transactions incentivized with cashback.
required_documents: Aadhaar Card, Vending Certificate/ID Card, Bank Account Passbook, Passport Photo
application_steps: 1. Apply via ULB or pmsvanidhi.mohua.gov.in. 2. Submit vending certificate and Aadhaar. 3. Loan sanctioned by lending institution. 4. Amount credited to bank account.

description: PMMY provides loans up to Rs. 10 lakh to non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises. Loans are classified as Shishu (up to Rs. 50,000), Kishore (Rs. 50,001 to Rs. 5 lakh), and Tarun (Rs. 5,00,001 to Rs. 10 lakh).
benefits: Collateral-free loans for micro enterprises. Shishu: up to Rs. 50,000; Kishore: Rs. 50,001-5,00,000; Tarun: Rs. 5,00,001-10,00,000. Available through banks, NBFCs, and MFIs.
required_documents: Aadhaar Card, Business Proof/Project Report, Bank Account, Identity Proof, Address Proof
application_steps: 1. Visit any bank/NBFC/MFI branch or mudra.org.in. 2. Submit business plan and KYC documents. 3. Loan processing and sanction. 4. Amount disbursed to account.

description: PMJDY is a National Mission for Financial Inclusion to ensure access to financial services namely Banking/Savings & Deposit Accounts, Remittance, Credit, Insurance, Pension in an affordable manner.
benefits: Zero balance bank account. RuPay debit card with accident insurance cover of Rs. 2 lakh. Overdraft facility up to Rs. 10,000. Life cover of Rs. 30,000 (if account opened before Jan 2015).
required_documents: Aadhaar Card or any officially valid document, Passport Photo
application_steps: 1. Visit any bank branch or Business Correspondent. 2. Fill account opening form. 3. Submit Aadhaar/valid ID. 4. Account opened instantly with RuPay card.

scheme_name: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA)
description: MGNREGA aims to enhance livelihood security in rural areas by providing at least 100 days of guaranteed wage employment in a financial year to every household whose adult members volunteer to do unskilled manual work.
benefits: 100 days of guaranteed wage employment per household per year. Minimum wage as per state rates. Unemployment allowance if work not provided within 15 days. Worksite facilities including crèche, drinking water, and shade.
required_documents: Aadhaar Card, Job Card (issued by Gram Panchayat), Bank Account Passbook, Passport Photo
application_steps: 1. Apply for Job Card at Gram Panchayat. 2. Submit application for work. 3. Work allocated within 15 days. 4. Wages paid within 15 days of work completion to bank/post account.

description: SSY is a small deposit scheme for the girl child launched as part of Beti Bachao Beti Padhao campaign. It encourages parents to build a fund for future education and marriage expenses of their girl child.
benefits: High interest rate (currently 8.2% p.a.). Tax benefits under Section 80C. Account matures after 21 years from opening or on marriage after age 18. Partial withdrawal allowed for education after age 18.
required_documents: Birth Certificate of Girl Child, Aadhaar of Parent/Guardian, Address Proof, Passport Photo
application_steps: 1. Visit post office or authorized bank branch. 2. Fill SSY account opening form. 3. Submit birth certificate and KYC of guardian. 4. Minimum deposit Rs. 250. 5. Account active for 21 years.

description: APY is a pension scheme focused on the unorganized sector workers. It provides guaranteed minimum pension of Rs. 1,000 to Rs. 5,000 per month after attaining age 60, depending on contributions made.
benefits: Guaranteed pension of Rs. 1,000-5,000/month after age 60. Government co-contribution of 50% of subscriber contribution (max Rs. 1,000/year) for 5 years for eligible subscribers who joined before March 2016.
required_documents: Aadhaar Card, Bank Account Passbook, Mobile Number
application_steps: 1. Visit bank/post office with savings account. 2. Fill APY registration form. 3. Auto-debit mandate for contributions. 4. PRAN generated. 5. Pension starts at age 60.

description: PMUY aims to safeguard the health of women and children by providing them with clean cooking fuel (LPG). It provides LPG connections to women from BPL households.
benefits: Free LPG connection with financial assistance of Rs. 1,600. First refill and hotplate provided. EMI facility for stove and refill cost. Reduces indoor air pollution and health hazards.
required_documents: Aadhaar Card, BPL Ration Card/SECC Data, Bank Account Passbook, Passport Photo
application_steps: 1. Apply at nearest LPG distributor or pmuy.gov.in. 2. Submit BPL proof and Aadhaar. 3. KYC verification. 4. LPG connection installed at home.

scheme_name: National Scholarship Portal - Pre-Matric Scholarship for SC Students
description: Pre-Matric Scholarship for SC students studying in Class 9 and 10. Implemented by Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment to support education of SC students from economically weaker sections.
benefits: Scholarship amount: Rs. 150/month for day scholars, Rs. 350/month for hostellers for 10 months. Additional allowance for disabled students. Books and ad-hoc grant of Rs. 750-1000 per annum.
required_documents: Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate (SC), Income Certificate, Bonafide Certificate, Bank Account Passbook
application_steps: 1. Register on scholarships.gov.in. 2. Fill application form online. 3. Upload documents. 4. Institute verification. 5. Scholarship credited to bank account.

scheme_name: National Scholarship Portal - Post-Matric Scholarship for OBC Students
description: Post-Matric Scholarship for OBC students for pursuing post-matriculation or post-secondary courses in recognized institutions. Covers maintenance allowance, tuition fees, and other compulsory fees.
benefits: Maintenance allowance: Rs. 260-750/month. Reimbursement of compulsory non-refundable fees. Study tour charges up to Rs. 1,600/year. Thesis typing/printing charges for research scholars.
required_documents: Aadhaar Card, OBC Certificate, Income Certificate, Mark Sheets, Bonafide Certificate, Bank Account Passbook
application_steps: 1. Register on scholarships.gov.in. 2. Apply under OBC Post-Matric scheme. 3. Upload required certificates. 4. Institute and State verification. 5. Scholarship disbursed.

description: Stand Up India Scheme facilitates bank loans between Rs. 10 lakh and Rs. 1 crore to at least one SC or ST borrower and at least one woman borrower per bank branch for setting up greenfield enterprises.
benefits: Composite loan between Rs. 10 lakh to Rs. 1 crore for greenfield enterprise. Covers 75% of project cost. Interest rate as per RBI guidelines. Handholding support through SIDBI.
required_documents: Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate, Project Report, Business Plan, Bank Account, Identity Proof
application_steps: 1. Apply online at standupmitra.in or visit bank branch. 2. Submit project report and caste/gender proof. 3. Loan appraisal by bank. 4. Sanction and disbursement.

description: NPS is a voluntary, defined contribution retirement savings scheme designed to enable systematic savings during the subscriber's working life. It is regulated by PFRDA.
benefits: Market-linked returns. Tax benefits under Section 80C and 80CCD(1B). Portable across jobs and locations. Partial withdrawal allowed for specific purposes. Annuity pension after retirement.
required_documents: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Bank Account Details, Passport Photo, Cancelled Cheque
application_steps: 1. Register at enps.nsdl.com or visit Point of Presence. 2. Fill registration form and choose fund manager. 3. Make initial contribution (min Rs. 500). 4. PRAN allotted.

description: PMVVY is a pension scheme for senior citizens aged 60 years and above, providing assured pension based on a guaranteed rate of return on purchase price. Implemented through LIC of India.
benefits: Assured pension at 7.4% per annum for 10 years. Minimum purchase price Rs. 1,56,658 for Rs. 1,000/month pension. Maximum purchase price Rs. 15 lakh. Loan facility up to 75% after 3 years.
required_documents: Aadhaar Card, Age Proof, Bank Account Details, Passport Photo
application_steps: 1. Visit LIC branch or agent. 2. Fill PMVVY application form. 3. Pay purchase price. 4. Pension credited monthly/quarterly/half-yearly/yearly to bank account.

scheme_name: Assistance to Disabled Persons for Purchase/Fitting of Aids and Appliances (ADIP)
description: ADIP scheme provides aids and assistive devices to persons with disabilities at no cost or at subsidized rates to enable them to become independent and improve their quality of life.
benefits: Free or subsidized aids and appliances: wheelchairs, tricycles, hearing aids, artificial limbs, crutches, Braille equipment, etc. Travel cost reimbursement. Escort allowance for accompanying person.
required_documents: Disability Certificate (40%+), Aadhaar Card, Income Certificate, Passport Photo, Medical Prescription
application_steps: 1. Apply at ALIMCO camp or District Disability Rehabilitation Centre. 2. Submit disability certificate and income proof. 3. Assessment by medical team. 4. Aid/appliance provided free or at subsidized cost.

scheme_name: PM Formalisation of Micro Food Processing Enterprises (PMFME)
description: PMFME scheme supports micro food processing enterprises through credit-linked subsidy, seed capital to SHGs, and support for marketing and branding of local products.
benefits: Credit-linked capital subsidy of 35% up to Rs. 10 lakh. Seed capital of Rs. 40,000 per SHG member. Support for common infrastructure, marketing, and training.
required_documents: Aadhaar Card, Udyam Registration, Bank Account, Project Report, FSSAI License (if applicable)
application_steps: 1. Register on pmfme.mofpi.gov.in. 2. Submit DPR and Udyam registration. 3. Appraisal by State Nodal Agency. 4. Subsidy released through bank.

description: KCC scheme provides farmers with timely access to credit for their cultivation and other needs including purchase of inputs, post-harvest expenses, and consumption requirements.
benefits: Flexible credit limit based on land holding and crop pattern. Interest subvention of 2% per annum. Additional 3% prompt repayment incentive. Coverage for crop insurance and personal accident insurance.
required_documents: Aadhaar Card, Land Records, Bank Account Passbook, Passport Photo, Crop Details
application_steps: 1. Apply at bank branch or through KCC portal. 2. Submit land records and crop details. 3. Credit limit assessed. 4. KCC issued with revolving credit facility.

description: BBBP is a tri-ministerial initiative of Women and Child Development, Health & Family Welfare and Human Resource Development to address declining Child Sex Ratio and promote education of girl child.
benefits: Awareness campaigns for girl child education. Financial incentives in participating states. Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana linkage. Improved access to education and healthcare for girls.
required_documents: Birth Certificate, Aadhaar Card, School Enrollment Certificate, Bank Account of Parent
application_steps: 1. Register at Anganwadi Centre or District Women & Child Development Office. 2. Enroll girl child in education programs. 3. Link with SSY account for financial benefits. 4. Participate in awareness programs.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Garib Kalyan Anna Yojana (PMGKAY)
description: PMGKAY provides free food grains to NFSA beneficiaries. Under this scheme, eligible beneficiaries receive 5 kg free food grains per person per month in addition to their regular NFSA entitlement.
benefits: 5 kg free food grains (rice/wheat) per person per month. Additional to regular NFSA quota. No cost to beneficiaries. Distributed through Fair Price Shops.
required_documents: Ration Card (NFSA), Aadhaar Card
application_steps: 1. Ensure name is in NFSA beneficiary list. 2. Visit Fair Price Shop with ration card. 3. Biometric authentication via e-POS. 4. Collect free food grains monthly.

scheme_name: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (IGNOAPS)
description: IGNOAPS is a non-contributory old age pension scheme under National Social Assistance Programme (NSAP) for persons aged 60 years and above living below poverty line.
benefits: Central assistance of Rs. 200/month for persons aged 60-79 years. Rs. 500/month for persons aged 80 years and above. State governments may add their contribution.
required_documents: Aadhaar Card, Age Proof, BPL Card/Income Certificate, Bank Account Passbook, Passport Photo
application_steps: 1. Apply at Block/District Social Welfare Office. 2. Submit age and BPL proof. 3. Verification by authorities. 4. Pension credited to bank/post account monthly.

description: PMEGP is a credit-linked subsidy programme for generation of employment opportunities through establishment of micro enterprises in rural and urban areas. Implemented by KVIC.
benefits: Margin money subsidy: 15-35% of project cost in urban areas, 25-35% in rural areas. Maximum project cost: Rs. 50 lakh (manufacturing), Rs. 20 lakh (service). EDP training provided.
education_requirement: Class 8 Pass (for projects above Rs. 10 lakh)
required_documents: Aadhaar Card, EDP Training Certificate, Project Report, Caste Certificate (if applicable), Bank Account, Passport Photo
application_steps: 1. Apply online at kviconline.gov.in/pmegpeportal. 2. Submit project report and EDP certificate. 3. Interview by District Task Force. 4. Bank loan and margin money subsidy.

description: NRLM (Aajeevika) aims to reduce poverty by enabling poor households to access gainful self-employment and skilled wage employment opportunities through self-managed SHGs and federations.
benefits: Revolving fund and community investment fund to SHGs. Interest subvention on bank loans. Capacity building and skill training. Market linkage support. Livelihood diversification.
required_documents: Aadhaar Card, BPL/SECC Data, Bank Account Passbook, Passport Photo
application_steps: 1. Contact Village Organization or Block Mission Management Unit. 2. Join Self Help Group. 3. Participate in SHG meetings and savings. 4. Access bank linkage and livelihood training.

description: PM CARES for Children scheme supports children who lost both parents or surviving parent/legal guardian/adoptive parents due to COVID-19 pandemic. Provides comprehensive support till age 23.
benefits: Fixed deposit of Rs. 10 lakh per child. Monthly stipend from 18-23 years. Free education in nearest Kendriya Vidyalaya or private school. Assistance for higher education. Health insurance under PM-JAY.
required_documents: Death Certificate of Parent(s), Aadhaar of Child and Guardian, Guardianship Certificate, Bank Account Details
application_steps: 1. Apply on pmcaresforchildren.in. 2. Submit death certificate and guardianship proof. 3. Verification by District Magistrate. 4. Benefits activated including FD and education support.
